pi_items.source: task.json line 17, task description line 6, export_result.sh line 16, verifier.py line 54 - all consistently reference 'generator_hours' as the target feed name

Mathematical verification: Goal is 1 hour running = 1.0 kWh. Status (0/1) scaled by 1000 yields 1000 watts. 1000 W × 1 hour = 1000 Wh = 1.0 kWh. Verified in verifier.py lines 9-12 (formula explanation) and line 63 (acceptance range 950-1050). Consistent with Emoncms Power to kWh process integration.

Mathematical calculation: 10 seconds = 10/3600 hours = 0.002777... hours. At 1000 watts (scaled status): 1000 W × (10/3600) h = 2.7777... Wh = 0.002777... kWh ≈ 0.00277 kWh. Used in verifier.py line 31 as expected_slope. Difference from true value 0.0027777... is only 0.0000078, well within tolerance.
task.json line 20, used in verifier.py line 32 and line 86. Tolerance range: [0.00227, 0.00327]. Reasonable for measurement variations in a simulated environment with finite time resolution.
setup_task.sh lines 43-45: Generator ON phase loops 4 times, sleeping 5 seconds each iteration = 20 seconds total
setup_task.sh lines 46-49: Generator OFF phase loops 4 times, sleeping 5 seconds each iteration = 20 seconds total
setup_task.sh lines 41-49: Infinite loop with 20s ON phase followed by 20s OFF phase

pi_items.verified_value: Emoncms - open-source web application for processing, logging, and visualising energy and environmental data. Part of OpenEnergyMonitor project.
pi_items.source: https://docs.openenergymonitor.org/emoncms/ - Confirmed as real software. env.json line 5 describes it. env.json uses openenergymonitor/emoncms:latest Docker image.

pi_items.verified_value: Emoncms input processor that integrates power (watts) over time to produce cumulative energy (kWh). Calculation: Energy(kWh) = Power(W) × Time(hours) / 1000
pi_items.source: Web search: https://docs.openenergymonitor.org/emoncms/daily-kwh.html confirms 'Power to kWh' input processor exists and accumulates kWh from power data. Verified in verifier.py lines 9-12 (mathematical explanation of the process).

task.json lines 3-6 description specifies feed creation with 'Tag: generator'

setup_task.sh line 33: Simulator posts json={generator_status:val}. Lines 59-68 verify input exists with name='generator_status'. export_result.sh line 35 queries for this input.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires configuring an Emoncms input processing chain to track cumulative generator running hours. The generator_status input (under generator_room node) receives synthetic binary data (1=running, 0=stopped) in alternating 20-second phases. The solution requires creating a generator_hours feed (PHPFina engine, 10-second interval, 'generator' tag) with a process chain: Scale process (factor ~1000) followed by Power to kWh integration. The scale factor converts the binary status to 1000 watts, which when integrated with the Power to kWh process yields approximately 0.00277 kWh per 10-second interval when running (mathematically: 1000 W × 10/3600 h ÷ 1000 = 0.002777... kWh). The test verifies the feed exists, contains correct processes, and increments at the expected rate (±0.0005 kWh tolerance).
